calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($7,975,646 USD / 4,826 total beneficiaries) = $1,652.64 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 0.605 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.
Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 36%, Unspecified: 64%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'However, 2023-24 was one of our worst years in recent memory for adoption with numbers falling significantly from 2022-23.'
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: Organisation provided euthanasia or live-release statistics.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

source.quote: Established in 1921, the Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (Hong Kong) ("SPCA" or "the Society") is the region's longest-standing and largest independent animal welfare charity.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total veterinary consultations provided to animals in Hong Kong during the reporting year.
Total animals receiving vaccinations through SPCA veterinary services in Hong Kong.
Total animals spayed/neutered through SPCA's desexing services and animal birth control schemes.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Euthanasia rates for cats and dogs would have been significantly higher without desexing programs.

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals rescued or handled by the Inspectorate Department in Hong Kong.
metrics.metric_name: Animals Rescued or Handled by Inspectorate

source.quote: 4,285 animals were rescued or handled by the Inspectorate Department.

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals adopted and placed in new loving homes in Hong Kong.

source.quote: 979 animals were adopted and became cherished family members of their new loving homes.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total animals euthanised by SPCA in Hong Kong.

source.quote: Firstly, we are focusing our advocacy efforts on the amendments to Cap. 169 Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Ordinance, which have been under discussion for a number of years.

significant_events.summary: Advocacy efforts focused on amendments to Cap. 169 Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Ordinance and regulation of trade under Cap. 139 Public Health (Animals and Birds) Ordinance.

source.quote: Our Animal Birth Control Programmes are critical in preventing and alleviating as much suffering as we can and addressing companion animal overpopulation at its root.

significant_events.summary: Continued and expanded desexing services through Cat Colony Care Programme and Community Dog Programme to control animal population.

significant_events.event_name: Opening of SPCA Jockey Club Centennial Centre

source.quote: The energy and excitement that we all felt culminated in the move to the SPCA Jockey Club Centennial Centre in Tsing Yi at the end of the calendar year for some of our staff, leading up to the grand opening in March 2024.

significant_events.summary: Grand opening of a new large-scale centre in Tsing Yi, expanding capacity for animal treatment, shelter, and education.

source.quote: Today, the 20-person team carries out a wide range of tasks which most famously includes a 24-hour rescue service.

significant_events.summary: The Inspectorate team carried out 24-hour rescue services, investigated cruelty cases, and assisted law enforcement.

source.quote: One of the most visible aspects of the SPCA is our work to find animals their forever homes.

significant_events.summary: Efforts to rehome abandoned and rescued animals, despite declining adoption rates, with new initiatives like the Adoption Date App.

source.quote: Pet health lies at the heart of what the SPCA does, and keeps us anchored in the roots of the organisation founded more than 120 years ago.

significant_events.summary: Rebuilding and expanding veterinary services, including consultations, surgeries, and specialized care, after staffing challenges.

source.quote: Educating the citizens of Hong Kong particularly children is a powerful way for the SPCA to achieve its long-term vision and mission.

significant_events.summary: Restructured education program with four pillars, including school engagement, public training, and an immersive classroom.

source.quote: There is vast amount of research and data to show the enormous mental and physical benefits that come from owning a pet.

significant_events.summary: Organized workshops and advocacy for pet-inclusivity, including 'Bring Your Dog to Work' programme and dog socialisation.

source.quote: The increasing connection between Hong Kong and Mainland China is proving a boon for people and the economy so it is only natural that the work of the SPCA should expand there too.

significant_events.summary: Expanded animal welfare education and knowledge exchange in Mainland China, including support for shelters and government advice.

source.quote: Reaching a new generation is also a key push, so to that end, the SPCA launched its Adoption Date App in February 2024.

significant_events.summary: Launched a mobile app to highlight animals available for adoption to a wider audience, with over 4,000 downloads.

source.quote: However, 2023-24 was one of our worst years in recent memory for adoption with numbers falling significantly from 2022-23.

data.original_text: The PSPCA's extraordinary work benefits animals in need, pet parents, and residents throughout Pennsylvania, crossing all social and economic boundaries and age ranges.

In 2020, ASPCA launched a series of programs in response to the COVID-19 pandemic and its effect on pets, owners, and communities including free pet food for dogs, cats, and horses in New York City, Los Angeles, Miami, and Asheville, grants to animal welfare organizations, emergency pet boarding services, a New York City COVID-19 Pet Hotline, and expanded stationary and mobile veterinary care.
A Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (SPCA) is a common name for non-profit animal welfare organizations around the world.
The PSPCA rescues animals from cruelty and neglect, rehabilitates them medically and behaviorally, and places them with new, loving families.

We bring our mission to life through the lifesaving efforts of our Animal Law Enforcement division, in-house Shelter Hospital, low-cost veterinary services, behavior and enrichment program, humane education, and placement of abandoned animals in loving forever homes.
Among its many programs are a 24-hour animal poison control hotline, animal training and behavior modification, and mobile spay and neuter clinics.
Donations run programs and services including Cruelty Investigations, Disaster Animal Response Team, Violence Prevention Humane Education and a variety of shelter services.
SPCA International distributes critical veterinary medical supplies to animal welfare groups throughout the world.
We provide grants to small shelters, rescue organizations and advocacy groups to support their life-saving work.
SPCA International delivers important, actionable information to pet parents, animal activists and concerned citizens to help improve the lives of animals around the world.
The PSPCA has the distinction of being the state's oldest, largest and most comprehensive animal welfare organization, and the nation's second oldest.
We do not euthanize for time or space, and we are proud to have achieved a 97 percent live-release rate.
In its first two years, the partnership increased the number of animal cruelty arrests in the city by more than 200 percent.
In 1966, the ASPCA helped pass the Animal Welfare Act (AWA).

The ASPCA has also been a leader in animal welfare. It pioneered the idea of cage-free shelters such as Maddie's Pet Adoption in San Francisco, California, where the animals live in a home-like setting until they are adopted.
The ASPCA has rescued countless dogs and puppies from puppy mills and worked to place them in shelters and rescues where they can be fostered until they are ready to be adopted into homes.
In the rescue mission, ASPCA granted more than $13 million to shelters and organizations in the region to help with the resettlement.
SPCA International has distributed millions of dollars in veterinary supplies and medication to organizations on almost every continent.
In 2023, the ASPCA awarded nearly $7.5 million in grants to 393 organizations across 49 states and the District of Columbia.
In 2023, we had over 43,000 enrollments from more than 700 organizations worldwide, sharing much of this expertise on ASPCApro.org.
In 2023, more than 600 animals were helped at the center.

In 2023, the ASPCA Animal Hospital provided veterinary care and critical services, including over 1,700 surgeries, for more than 9,700 pets who were victims of cruelty and neglect or whose owners experienced financial challenges.

We partner with shelters and rescues across 40 states to move more than 20,000 animals each year from shelters in areas with high populations of homeless pets to destination shelters where there is a greater demand for adoptable animals.
Since 2014, the ASPCA Animal Relocation & Placement program has relocated more than 250,000 animals.
The ASPCA opens a dedicated Kitten Nursery in New York City, primarily for kittens relinquished to Animal Care Centers of NYC, saving 11,000 kittens over 10 years.
The ASPCA helps pass the Veterinary Medicine Mobility Act, which enables mobile and ambulatory veterinarians to offer lifesaving care to animals in crisis.
The ASPCA launches a Behavioral Rehabilitation Center pilot program for fearful dogs from puppy mills and hoarding situations.
successful adoption drives where hundreds of pets find forever homes within days!
Our puppy mill campaign has increased enforcement action and reduced the number of breeding dogs kept in U.S. Department of Agriculture-licensed mills by one-third. That's 32,000 dogs a year!
We've also driven the passage of seven state and nearly 500 local ordinances to stop the sale of puppies in stores.

Over the last half century, we've helped to substantially drive down the numbers of animals who have entered shelters and, in turn, those who are at risk of euthanasia, through education, sterilization and adoption initiatives.
But last year, South Korea passed a law to end the dog meat trade.
We have won more than 100 favorable rulings for animals and drafted and defended countless new state and local laws and regulations.
And through ballot measure campaigns in the U.S., we helped the pro-animal position prevail through direct votes in more than two-thirds of 61 initiatives.

name: Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals
